title: Everest’s Tweaks
description: Everest's tweaks mod used on Aconite SMP
body:
### A FabricMC tweaks mod with a focus on bundles.
The mod doesn't add things directly, instead improves the games features, for example, by making bundles fully configureable.

# Current Features
### Bundle Changes
- Removed the ability to drop all  items in bundles
- Fully configurable amount of unstackable items weight
- Fully configurable amount of totems weight
- Fully configurable amount of enchanting book weight 
- Fully configurable max bundle volume, max 128
### Recipes
- 1 leather, 1 string bundle recipe
- Rotten flesh smelts into leather
- Leather can be smelted into rabbit hide
- Deepslate recipe, 2 tuff, 2 stone
- Cobbled deepslate can be made into deepslate
- Calcite can be crafted with coral
- Gravel crafts into flint
- Raw iron blocks can be smelted / blasted into iron blocks 
- Raw gold blocks can be smelted / blasted into gold blocks
- Chains are cheaper
- Nametag with string and paper
### Tag Changes
- Beacons are now broken with a pickaxe ( any level )
- Glass is now broken with a pickaxe ( any level )

# FAQ
**Q: Can I port this mod to Forge?**

**A: Yes, but** you must provide credits to me, and I will not answer questions about the Forge port.

**Q: Can I port this mod to Bedrock?**

**A: Yes**, if you figure out how to do that, we do not have an issue with it. We only ask to please do credit the artists and coders who worked on the mod.

**Q: Can you backport this mod to 1.x? When will you update to 1.x?**

**A: No.** We mod Minecraft as a hobby with very limited time. Therefore, we will most likely not backport any of our mods to older versions of Minecraft, unless we actually have a need for it. We will update it to future versions when we feel like it, don't come begging.

**Q: Can I include this mod in a modpack?**

**A: Yes you can.** Go ahead, don't bother asking. Please however provide credit or link to the Modrinth project page.
